RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 109-D, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Tonya Rush, a Democrat, proudly represents part of St. Louis County (District 67) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Rush attended Normandy High School and has spent over 14 years as a dedicated healthcare provider. She is also a licensed Realtor in the state of Missouri. Her career reflects her commitment to serving others and improving the well-being of her community.
A family-oriented individual, Rep. Rush has been happily married to her husband, Terrell, for 25 years. Together, they have two adult children, Lakeah and Terrell Jr., who are an important part of her life and values.
Rep. Rush’s experience in healthcare and her strong family foundation guide her work in the legislature as she strives to make a positive impact on her district and the state of Missouri.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Private Security Camera Incentive Program", within the department of public safety
Modifies provisions relating to licensure requirements for home health agencies
Creates provisions relating to temporary real estate salesperson licenses
Requires school districts to provide period products at no cost in charter schools and public middle and high schools
Establishes a program for providing paid leave for parents working in a child's school and authorizes a tax credit for fifty percent of the hourly wage paid to a participant in the program
Modifies provisions governing mental health efforts in public schools
Modifies the offense of unlawful possession of a firearm to include any minor possessing a firearm on public property

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Missouri Dignity in Pregnancy and Childbirth Act"
Modifies financial requirements of the radioactive waste investigation fund
Establishes the "Office of the Department of Corrections Ombudsman" and provisions relating to department of corrections oversight
Adds improperly transporting or storing a firearm in a motor vehicle to the offense of unlawful use of a weapon
Authorizes the "Volunteer First Responder Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for certain first responders
Creates provisions relating to workplace security for health care professionals
Requires school districts to provide instruction in cursive writing
Requires certain police departments to establish a missing person's unit
Extends the expiration date of a provision allowing retired teachers to serve as substitute teachers without a discontinuance of such teacher's retirement allowance
Creates new provisions relating to joint employers
Establishes provisions relating to a freestanding claim of actual innocence in a postconviction action or proceeding challenging the validity of a judgment
Designates certain actions by employers, when based on employees' credit history or credit scores, as improper employment practices
Modifies provisions governing student attendance data reported for school accreditation processes
Authorizes the use of moneys in Missouri empowerment scholarship accounts for driver education purposes
Provides for mileage reimbursements for dual enrollment courses for college credit
Establishes additional requirements for computer science courses in schools
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
